jamaican-oxtail-stew.name: Jamaican Oxtail Stew
instructions: Clean and trim excess fat off oxtails. Stem and seed habanero peppers.
Combine 1 habanero pepper, onions, bell peppers, scallions, ginger, thyme, garlic, and bouillon in the bowl of a food processor. Blend to a paste-like consistency.
Rub oxtails with 1/2 cup of paste, jerk seasoning, browning sauce, and soy sauce in a large bowl. Massage mixture deep into oxtails. Cover, refrigerate, and marinate 8 hours or overnight.
Remove from refrigerator and let stand until room temperature, about 30 minutes. Remove oxtails from marinade. Reserve marinade.

Heat oil in a large pot with a tight-fitting lid over medium heat. Add oxtails, browning on all sides, about 6 minutes. Add reserved marinade and ketchup. Cover and cook until warmed through, about 15 minutes. Add water, remaining habanero pepper, allspice, and bay leaf. Cook over medium heat until meat is tender, stirring often to prevent sticking, 2 to 3 hours.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Reduce heat and let simmer, 15 to 20 minutes. Add beans and tomatoes and simmer until heated through, 5 to 10 minutes more.

jans-dark-chocolate-swirl-pound-cake.name: Jan's Dark Chocolate Swirl Pound Cake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C). Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan.

Beat butter and cream cheese together in a bowl using an electric mixer until well combined; beat in sugar until fluffy, 3 to 4 minutes. Beat in almond extract, vanilla extract, salt, and baking powder; beat in 2 eggs. Add 1 cup flour; mix well. Add 2 eggs; mix well. Add 1 cup flour; mix well. Add remaining 2 eggs; mix well. Add remaining 1 cup flour; mix until batter is smooth.

Transfer 2 cups batter to a separate bowl; stir in cocoa powder. Pour 1/2 plain batter into the prepared pan; add 1/2 chocolate batter and swirl with a knife. Repeat with remaining batters, swirling gently.
Bake on the middle rack of the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, about 90 minutes.

janeys-vegetable-noodle-soup.name: Janey's Vegetable Noodle Soup
instructions: Bring water and olive oil to a boil in a pot over medium-high heat. Add noodles. Cook, stirring occasionally, until mostly tender, about 3 minutes. Add vegetable broth and carrot; cook and stir for 1 minute. Add green bell pepper, celery, and soy sauce; stir and bring to a boil for 1 minute more. Sprinkle green onions on top.

jenga-ribs.name: Jenga Ribs
instructions: Whisk garlic powder, salt, brown sugar, paprika, chili powder, pepper, cumin, onion powder, cinnamon, and coriander in a small bowl to blend. DO AHEAD: Spice blend can be made 1 month ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

Preheat oven to 300°. Rub ribs all over with spice blend and wrap each rack individually in foil, crimping seams to seal tightly. Place both racks on a rimmed baking sheet. Bake ribs until fork-tender, about 3 hours. DO AHEAD: Ribs can be made 2 days ahead. Open foil packets and let ribs cool completely. Rewrap tightly and refrigerate on same baking sheet.
Preheat oven to 425°. Cut racks between bones into individual ribs. Divide ribs between 2 foil-lined rimmed baking sheets and bake until heated through and golden brown, 15-20 minutes.
Layer ribs on plates in alternating directions, 3 ribs per layer. Serve ribs, passing barbecue sauce alongside.

judys-brown-sauce.name: Judy's Brown Sauce

ingredients.prep: cored, and roughly chopped tart apples

ingredients.item: Pitted and halved fresh prune plums
Half-pint canning jars with lids and rings

instructions: Place apples, plums, and onions into the bowl of a food processor, working in batches; pulse until fine. Transfer ground fruit and onions to a large pot; stir in malt vinegar, sugar, salt, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, ginger, nutmeg, allspice, and cayenne pepper until well combined. Bring to a simmer over low heat until brown and thickened, 3 to 4 hours.
Inspect 15 half-pint jars for cracks and rings for rust, discarding any defective ones. Immerse in simmering water until brown sauce is ready. Wash new, unused lids and rings in warm soapy water.

Pack sauce into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within ¼ inch of tops. Run a clean knife or thin spatula around insides of jars to remove any air bubbles. Wipe rims with a moist paper towel to remove any residue. Top with lids and screw rings on tightly.

Place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot and fill halfway with water. Bring to a boil and lower jars 2 inches apart into the boiling water using a holder. Pour in more boiling water to cover jars by at least 1 inch. Bring to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 10 minutes.

Remove jars from stockpot; place on a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart, until cool. Press centers of lids with a finger to ensure lids do not move up or down. Store in a cool, dark area.

juvetsi-fish-argiro.name: Juvetsi Fish

ingredients.item: 400 grish fillet (whistle, phakry or whatever you like) in pieces
1 ltwater or broth of fish or vegetarians
400 grish fillet (whistle, phakry or whatever you like) in pieces
1 ltwater or broth of fish or vegetarians

instructions: Finely chop the vegetarians into small frame, onion, carrot leek and celery.
Heat half the oil in a wide pan and add the vegetarians.
Sauté for 3’.
Put them in a small baking pan.
Add the barley and the hot broth.
Touch the fish pieces into the materials and press them into the liquids.
Add salt and pepper and pour with the remaining olive oil.
Sprinkle with the fresh onions and baking in a pre-heated oven on the bottom grill for 15’.
We're touching the shripp on the juvechi.
Sprinkle with herbs and continue baking for 5’ – 6’ extra.
Finally, squeeze the lemon into the fish stew and let the food stand for 5’.
Serve it warm while it's still duty.
